books.title: Drawing, Design, and Craft-Work
books.author: Frederick J. Glass
books.rationale: This is an excellent guide to art instruction, with suggestions for how to begin, materials to use, and the directions future work may take. This book is profusely illustrated with clear explanations.
books.noteText: The free link is a different edition, and the lesson plans will have different page numbers. Download the file to view the free version. It will likely appear blank in the PDF reader.

Art: Level 2
Art: Level 3

books.title: Brush Drawing as Applied to Natural Forms and Common Objects
books.author: May Mallam
books.rationale: This is a complete guide to learning how to brush-draw. Exercises progress from simple lines to monochromatic value painting to painting in color. Techniques taught help students learn to "see" so that they can visually represent their observations.
books.sharedText:
Art: Level 4
Art: Level 5

books.title: Redeeming Vision
books.author: Elissa Weichbrodt
books.rationale: Educators of all levels and high school students take the next step in deepening their relationship with art by contemplating a theology of art for viewers which impacts how we see as well as how we are and how we love.  Practice in taking what has been learned in previous years along with new aspects of art elements and themes is guided by Dr. Weichbrodt.

books.title: Art Print Set
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This art print set contains high-resolution images that are in the public domain or used for Alveary with permission. The Art Print Set contains paintings by each term's artist: Edward Mitchell Bannister, Vincent van Gogh, and Lilias Trotter. We recommend printing on cardstock. Hard copies will be available with lesson plans.
books.noteText: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12.

books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

books.title: A Passion for the Impossible: The Life of Lilias Trotter
books.author: Miriam Rockness
books.rationale: An inspiring biography of Lilias Trotter, a British artist who forsook fame to devote her life to Christian missionary work in Algeria, guided by faith, creativity, and love.

books.title: Van Gogh
books.author: Ingo Walther & Rainer Metzger
books.rationale: This comprehensive study of Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) pairs a detailed monograph on his life and art with a complete catalogue of his 871 paintings.
books.noteText: Please note there is some minor nudity, and Van Gogh lived a tumultuous life, ultimately committing suicide.

books.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
books.author: Tim Dowley
books.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.
books.maySubText: other Bible atlas, though page numbers in lesson plans will not match.

books.title: The New Manners and Customs of Bible Times
books.author: Ralph Gower
books.rationale: Through illustrations, photos, and clear explanations, this best-selling reference book shows what life was like in Bible times and makes Scripture come to life as students imagine living in that time and place. We will assign certain sections before or after Bible readings that will help students understand the context of the passage and gain more insight into the culture and geography of Israel.
books.sharedText:

books.title: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China
books.author: Janet & Geoff Benge
books.rationale: Hudson Taylor (1832-1905), the founder of China Inland Mission, continues to inspire through the story of his love for the people of China and his trust in the Lord to provide financially for his ministry. Taylor faced persecution and loss in his own life in order to bring the gospel message to the “ripe harvest fields in China.”

books.title: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor
books.author: Dave & Neta Jackson
books.rationale: Born in Scotland, Mary Slessor (1848-1915) became a missionary to Calabar, Africa (now Southern Nigeria) at the age of 27. As she moved further inland, she brought the gospel to unreached people and rescued many twin babies, who otherwise would have been killed because of the mistaken beliefs of their parents.

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.
books.sharedText:

books.author: Emily Beesly
books.rationale: This is a narrative introduction to the stories students will read later in Plutarch's Lives.
books.noteText: Contains some violence that may be disturbing for sensitive children.

books.noteText: Used with publisher's permission.

books.title: Grammar Island
books.author: Michael Clay Thompson
books.rationale: This program brings story, delight, and ideas to the study of grammar. Grammar Island introduces, in a simple fashion, the full four-level grammar analysis of (1) parts of speech, (2) parts of the sentence, (3) phrases, and (4) clauses.

books.title: Italics: Beautiful Handwriting for Children
books.author: Penny Gardner
books.rationale: We like this model because it is lovely, yet simple. Both the print and cursive models are in italic, making this a solid foundation for fine lettering later. Some may prefer a more traditional cursive model.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series.
books.maySubText: penmanship model of your choice or Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series

books.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book E
books.author: Handwriting Success
books.rationale: While practicing cursive italic, students explore the many facets of the world of science — the animal kingdom, DNA, photosynthesis, minerals, continents, biomes, our solar system, and galaxy. Practice with capitals: cities of the world and history of letters.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.
books.maySubText: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner

books.title: Student World Atlas 6th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
books.maySubText: other atlases; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.

Story of Arithmetic: Part 1
Story of Arithmetic: Part 2

books.title: David Livingstone: African Explorer
books.author: John Tiner
books.rationale: Students will be intrigued by David Livingstone's (1813-1873) adventurous spirit, near-death experiences, as well as the flora and fauna of Africa. This book begins with David in his home country of Scotland and tells how he made his way to Africa. Our lesson plans provide map drills and mapping activities not only about Africa, but  also about how to create other kinds of maps based on one’s room or neighborhood.

books.title: Africa Is Not a Country
books.author: Margy Burns Knight
books.rationale: This nonfiction picture book introduces students to the diversity of the African continent. Using photographs and short descriptions, it shows students that Africa is made up of many different countries, cultures, languages, landscapes, and ways of life. It will be read slowly throughout the year to help students get a better picture of modern-day Africa.

books.title: SeaMan: The Dog Who Explored The West With Lewis & Clark
books.author: Gail Langer Karwoski
books.rationale: The exciting adventures of Lewis and Clark are captured in this engaging book. This fictional account is full of accurate details drawn from Lewis’s own diary entries. This book provides students an interesting and accessible way to understand the challenges, dangers, and daily life of the Lewis and Clark expedition.

books.title: Heart and Soul: The Story of America and African Americans
books.author: Kadir Nelson
books.rationale: This book is written in the voice of an old African American grandmother taking a child on her knee to tell the stories of the black experience throughout American history. A couple of chapters are read each term. It will also be used the following year.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: Lincoln: A Photobiography
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: This book follows the events leading up to, during, and after the Civil War through the life of Abraham Lincoln.  As students read about Lincoln’s personal and public struggles, students will learn about the character and leadership of this man during one of the most tumultuous times of our nation’s history. This is the first war during which photography was used to show the people back home what war was really like, so the images capture not only the events, but also the new technology of the age.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Children of the Wild West
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: The author does an excellent job of showing what it was like for pioneer children to experience life in the 1800's. Many fascinating photographs are included that help students see how different life was back then. He also tells about what life was like for some Native American children who had to navigate boarding schools and give up their traditional ways.
books.noteText: Available with Audio on Learning Ally.

books.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
books.author: Derick Bingham
books.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

books.title: Under the Hawthorn Tree
books.author: Marita Conlon-McKenna
books.rationale: This book is a fictional account of a family during the 1840s when Ireland was devastated by the potato famine. Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. This is an important event that helps students understand why so many Irish immigrants came to the US during this time period.

books.title: Commodore Perry in the Land of the Shogun
books.author: Rhoda Blumberg
books.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 2-4
Tall Tales/ Fables

books.title: The Kids Book of Canadian History
books.author: Carlotta Hacker
books.rationale: This book brings Canada's history to life with engaging illustrations, helpful maps, and a narrative that keeps the complex events of our nation's past straightforward enough for even the youngest historians.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: Laura Secord's Brave Walk
books.author: Connie Brummel Crook
books.rationale: Laura Secord's Brave Walk introduces young readers to the War of 1812 through the courage of one Canadian, who embarked on a long journey through a forest at night to deliver a message to the British troops. The engaging narrative and illustrations bring this episode of Canadian history to life for young readers.

books.title: Belle of Batoche
books.author: Jacqueline Guest
books.rationale: Belle of Batoche is a work of historical fiction which imagines one girl's experience in the Battle of Batoche in 1885, where Métis fighters led by Louis Riel raised an ultimately unsuccessful defence against Canadian militia troops in Batoche, Saskatchewan. A simple story of two girls' rivalry and eventual friendship is woven into an historically-accurate depiction both of life in the late-nineteenth century Canadian prairies and of specific events from the battle itself.
books.noteText: This is good as an independent read for Grade 4 or confident readers.

books.title: Born!: A Foal, Five Kittens and Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: A delightful story about the everyday life of twin siblings in Charlottetown during the Charlottetown Conference of 1864. Readers will learn about everyday life in nineteenth-century British North America as well as the events and political figures who were key to establishing Canada's Confederation.

books.title: I Am Not a Ghost: The Canadian Pacific Railway
books.author: David Bouchard
books.rationale: This beautifully-illustrated book tells the story of Chinese railway workers whose contributions to the building of the Canadian Pacific Railway were essential, yet who faced racism, hardships, exploitation, and even death. At the heart of this story is friendship and hope, when one gravely ill Chinese man is saved and cared for by Amelia Douglas, a Métis woman who was the wife of the first governor of British Columbia, as well as a trained nurse and midwife.

books.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
books.author: Derick Bingham
books.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

books.title: Under the Hawthorn Tree
books.author: Marita Conlon-McKenna
books.rationale: This book is a fictional account of a family during the 1840s when Ireland was devastated by the potato famine. Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. This is an important event that helps students understand why so many Irish immigrants came to the US during this time period.

books.title: Commodore Perry in the Land of the Shogun
books.author: Rhoda Blumberg
books.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.

books.title: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades
books.author: Ednah Rich
books.rationale: This book begins with very simple models; difficulty increases gradually.
books.noteText: Be sure to purchase the Yesterday's Classics edition, as other editions have issues with formatting and readability. Video tutorials are provided with lesson plans.
books.sharedText:
Sloyd: Level 1
Sloyd: Level 2
Sloyd: Level 3

books.title: The Ultimate Sashiko Sourcebook
books.author: Susan Briscoe
books.rationale: Students will use the illustrations and directions in the book to learn Sashiko techniques and designs.

books.title: The Amazing Stitching Handbook for Kids: 17 Embroidery Stitches
books.author: Kristin Nicholas
books.rationale: This book contains embroidery stitches that can be added to sewing projects.
books.maySubText: another sewing book.

books.title: Typing.com
books.rationale: This program uses fun activities and games to teach keyboarding fluency.
books.noteText: Create a free account.

books.title: In the Days of Giants
books.author: Abbie Farwell Brown
books.rationale: The Greeks were not the only ones with a story tradition that influenced Western culture. These Norse stories have also been highly influential, and Brown has retold them beautifully.

books.title: Russian Fairy Tales
books.author: Alexander Afanasyev
books.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.
books.noteText: Although the free and hard copy editions are not identical, they are from the same translator with nearly identical language. The tales scheduled in the lesson plans are included in both texts and are only scheduled for a few weeks.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: South American Jungle Tales
books.author: Horacio Quiroga
books.rationale: A Uruguayan author and playwright who lived at the end of the 19th century, Quiroga is sometimes compared to Rudyard Kipling and Edgar Allen Poe.
books.noteText: Students only read a tale or two from this collection, so the free version is recommended.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Short Stories & Folktales
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4

books.title: The Tales of Uncle Remus: The Adventures of Brer Rabbit
books.author: Julius Lester
books.rationale: Whether he is besting Brer Fox or sneaking into Mr. Man's garden, Brer Rabbit demonstrates that brains are better than brawn, along with other life lessons. These classic tales from the African American tradition are full of humor and creativity, and Julius Lester's retelling makes them a delight for readers today.

books.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
books.author: C. S. Lewis
books.rationale: This piece of classic literature by C.S. Lewis was chosen to foster students' moral formation and love for a well-told story.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.maySubText: another in the Narnia series or a title in adjacent grades if students have already read The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe

books.title: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates
books.author: Mary Mapes Dodge
books.rationale: Mary Mapes Dodge was recognized as one of the leading authors of children's literature during the 19th century, and Hans Brinker is her most famous work. With themes of perseverance and selflessness, Hans Brinker's heartwarming tale of two siblings delights, instructs, and expands the imagination.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grade 4
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.rationale: Hearing a play being performed often helps students with comprehension. These professional recordings are superb.
books.maySubText: Librivox recording.

books.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: One of Shakespeare’s most popular plays, A Midsummer Night’s Dream is a comedy set in Greece during the 12th century. The magical setting of this play is the city of Athens and the nearby enchanted woods where fairies and their mischief reign in the moonlight.
books.noteText: This is a good play for those who are newer to Shakespeare.

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: Over the River and Through the Wood: An Anthology of Nineteenth-Century American Children's Poetry
books.author: Karen L. Kilcup (Editor) & Angela Sorby (Editor)
books.rationale: A beautiful collection of 19th century children's poetry for all ages.
books.noteText: Grades 5-6 can substitute A Poem for Every Day of the Year edited by Allie Esiri if desired.
books.maySubText: Public domain links
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8

books.title: Gladiola Garden
books.author: Effie Lee Newsome
books.rationale: This book by a lesser-known American poet contains beautiful nature poetry and illustrations for children.

books.title: Emma's Poem: The Voice of the Statue of Liberty
books.author: Linda Glaser
books.rationale: This delightful picture book tells the fascinating story of the poem engraved on the Statue of Liberty.

books.title: RightStart Mathematics Level D 2nd Ed. Book Bundle
books.rationale:
RightStart is based on discovery, using a wide range of manipulatives. It is an up-to-date, spiraling program that uses methods that are heavily supported by current research. 
➜  Students also need the RS2 Math Set.
books.noteText:
The "Free" link will take you to a YouTube playlist where a master teacher will show you how to teach these lessons. 
eWorkbooks are available for multiple students.

books.title: Math Card Games Kit
books.author: RightStart
books.rationale:
Kit is recommended for those who do not use RightStart Math; kit is included in the RS2 Math Set. 
➜  Additional recommended game titles will be listed with the Lesson Plans for each level of mathematics.
books.maySubText: any games that allow students to practice math concepts and skills.

books.title: Linnea in Monet's Garden
books.author: Christina Björk
books.rationale: This beautifully illustrated children’s book introduces readers to art, history, travel, and the life of the famous Impressionist painter Claude Monet through the eyes of a curious young girl. The watercolor paintings and reproductions of Monet’s work provide a great way for students to learn French vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

books.title: French: Student Notebook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This notebook contains markable copies of lesson readings, vocabulary lists, and lyrics for songs.
books.noteText: This notebook is also available as part of hard-copy lessons in the print store.

books.title: Diez Deditos (Audio mp3)
books.author: Jose-Luis Orozco
books.rationale: This is a collection of traditional songs in Spanish.
books.noteText: Option 1 is a link to stream with Spotify, or Option 2 is a link to purchase the CD.
books.maySubText: favorite streaming service

books.title: Diez Deditos and Other Play Rhymes and Action Songs from Latin America
books.author: José-Luis Orozco
books.rationale: This is a collection of finger rhymes and action songs in Spanish.

books.title: Family Pictures/Cuadros de familia
books.author: Carmen Lomas Garza
books.rationale: The vibrant paintings and warm personal stories depict memories of the author growing up in a traditional Mexican-American family. This book is a great way to learn Spanish vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

books.title: Spanish: Student Notebook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This notebook contains markable copies of lesson readings, vocabulary lists, and answers to reading questions and grammar exercises.
books.noteText: This notebook is available as a hard copy with Spanish lessons in the Alveary print store.

books.title: Hymns, Spirituals, & Folk Songs PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12. Audio lessons and other resources are available in lesson plans.

books.title: Our Work: Grade 3
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.
books.sharedText:
Picture Study
Recitation: Grade 3
Music: Level 3

books.title: Our Work: Grades 4-6
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

books.title: Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition
books.author: Anna Harwell Celenza
books.rationale: This book tells the sad but touching story behind Mussorgysky's famous composition to honor his friend's paintings.
books.noteText: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. This book is optional and is not required to complete the lessons.

books.title: Song in a Rainstorm: The Story of Musical Prodigy Thomas "Blind Tom" Wiggins
books.author: Glenda Armand
books.rationale: "Had he been born in our time," the author writes, "he might have been called an autistic musical savant." Students will enjoy this picture book biography of an incredible American performer and composer during the last half of the 19th century.
books.noteText: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. Blind Tom is recommended for younger students.

books.title: Our Hymns, Our Heritage
books.author: David & Barbara Leeman
books.rationale: This student hymnal contains multi-denominational hymns organized by the church year, with brief histories of the music and lyrics, bios of the writers, related Scripture, and other ideas to meditate upon.

books.title: Like a Bird: The Art of the American Slave Song
books.author: Cynthia Grady
books.rationale: This is a collection of African American spirituals, along with their stories and beautiful illustrations.
books.noteText: 1-2 songs from this book are chosen most years.

books.title: Fifty Steps in Sight-Singing
books.author: Arthur Somervell
books.rationale: Classic text to teach sight-singing.
books.sharedText:
Sol-fa: Level 3
Sol-fa: Level 4
Sol-fa: Level 5

books.title: Sol-fa Level 3 Audio Lessons
books.author: Alveary
books.noteText: These will also be linked in the lesson plans. These will be available June 1.

books.title: The Ultimate Homeschool Physical Education Game Book
books.author: Guy Bailey
books.rationale: This book contains time-honored and newer playground games and skill-building activities for organized sports.
books.maySubText: The Physical Educator's Big Book of Sport Lead-Up Games if you have a co-op or school.
books.sharedText:
Playground Games
Sports

books.title: The Ultimate Homeschool Physical Education Game Book
books.author: Guy Bailey
books.rationale: This book contains time-honored and newer playground games and skill-building activities for organized sports.
books.maySubText: The Physical Educator's Big Book of Sport Lead-Up Games if you have a co-op or school.
books.sharedText:
Playground Games
Sports

books.title: 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games
books.author: Kimberly Wechsler
books.rationale: This book provides a great list of exercises that includes strength training, flexibility exercises, and cardiovascular activities. This can be used in a group or with one student.

books.title: The Book of Song Dances
books.author: John Feierabend
books.rationale: This book uses traditional songs, rhymes, and dances to teach children basic dance steps.
books.noteText: Instructional videos will be available June 1.
books.maySubText: earlier edition, but some dances have different page numbers.

books.title: The Universe in You: A Microscopic Journey
books.author: Jason Chin
books.rationale: This stunning picture book introduces students to Things-too-small-to-see.  With beautiful artwork and accessible language, this title is sure to inspire everyone to look more closely.

books.title: To Fly: The Story of the Wright Brothers
books.author: Wendie Old
books.rationale: This book tells the story of an important technology that students have probably observed and maybe even used. Along the way, they learn how the Wright Brothers were driven by curiosity and inspiration and how they persisted to solve one of the biggest challenges of their day.
books.maySubText: First to Fly by Peter Busby

books.title: The Elephant Scientist
books.author: Caitlin O'Connell and Donna M. Jackson
books.rationale: Challenges arise when wild animals meet human communities. The scientist in this story not only observes elephants, but she also uses what she learns from them, along with what she already knows about sound waves, to collaborate with the local community to navigate such challenges.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: A World in a Drop of Water: Exploring with a Microscope
books.author: Alvin and Virginia Silverstein
books.rationale: This book introduces students to the microscopic creatures that live in natural water. They will examine pond water under a microscope to see the hidden lives of single-celled organisms.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: The Octopus Scientists: Exploring the Mind of a Mollusk
books.author: Sy Montgomery
books.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at a creature from the family Mollusca. With respect and persistence, the author pursues the mysterious and fascinating octopus - and shares all that she learns with us, of course!
books.noteText: Available on Audible and Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Life on Surtsey: Iceland's Upstart Island
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: In 1963, a volcano erupted and created a new island off the coast of Iceland. This book tells the story of the scientists who are watching and recording life unfold through primary succession.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Science: Grade 4 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 4, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 4 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book.  It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term. 

NOTE: Also included in Grade 4 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.title: US Wall Map (US students only)
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any US wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.

books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.
books.sharedText:

books.title: World Wall Map
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: The projection of this wall map distorts the image less than others. It also makes geographical features easy to find and place names easy to read.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any world wall map that shows both geographical features and place names
books.sharedText:

Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Globe
books.rationale: We like that this globe is up to date and has raised landform features.
books.maySubText: any globe that shows both geographical features and place names
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6

books.title: Handbook of Nature Study
books.author: Anna Comstock
books.rationale: A must-have for nature study and outdoor object lessons, this book is incorporated into teacher preparation for science lessons. Older children may read for themselves as they find things they are interested in on their walks.
books.maySubText: the full-color seven-volume set from Living Books Press
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Nature Journaling for a Wild Life
books.author: Roseann Beggy Hanson
books.rationale: Explore the enriching world of nature journaling in an eight-week, self-guided “workshop in a book” from explorer, naturalist, and artist Roseann Hanson.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: The Naturalist's Notebook
books.author: Nathaniel T. Wheelwright & Bernd Heinrich
books.rationale: Become a more attentive observer and deepen your appreciation for the natural world. The unique five-year calendar format of _The Naturalist’s Notebook_ helps you create a long-term record and point of comparison for memorable events.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Tasting History
books.author: Max Miller
books.rationale: Including the original recipe and Miller’s modern recreation, this cookbook is a must-have for any avid cook or history fan looking to experience delicious recipes from the past.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Baking Class
books.author: Deanna Cook
books.rationale: This book teaches the secrets of great baking, using kid-friendly directions and recipes.

books.title: Curwen Music
books.author: Mrs. Curwen & Kelli Rummel
books.rationale: These are free video piano lessons for beginners that use the Curwen method.
books.noteText: Preliminary and part of First Step lessons are available online; higher levels are incomplete.
books.maySubText:
local piano lessons. Curwen teacher preferred
➜ May also sub Hoffman Academy

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
Food Science Lessons

books.title: The LEGO® Architect
books.author: Tom Alphin
books.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Woodshop 101 For Kids
books.author: Craig Stevens
books.rationale: Twenty-one woodworking lessons that teach the basics of woodworking.

books.title: The LEGO® Engineer
books.author: Jeff Friesen
books.rationale: LEGO® bricks meet _The Way Things Work_ in this fun, informative tour of the world of engineering, from the creative mind of expert LEGO® builder Jeff Friesen.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Simply Clean: The Proven Method for Keeping Your Home Organized, Clean, and Beautiful in Just 10 Minutes a Day
books.author: Becky Rapinchuk
books.rationale: This is a resource for parents who would like some help developing a cleaning rhythm in their homes. It includes step-by-step instructions on how to clean and perform other household tasks.

books.title: Roots, Shoots, Buckets, & Boots: Activities to Do in the Garden
books.author: Sharon Lovejoy
books.rationale: This book includes lots of ideas for beginner gardeners that will be a joy to both you and your students.

books.title: Cooking Class
books.author: Deanna Cook
books.rationale: This book teaches basic food preparation and cooking, using kid-friendly directions and recipes.

books.title: Coding for Kids: Python: Learn to Code with 50 Awesome Games and Activities
books.author: Adrienne Tacke
books.rationale: This book includes games and activities that teach kids to code with Python.

books.title: GeoPuzzles
books.rationale: Kids will learn geography in a fun and engaging way, while discussing the different countries and how they are related.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Coding & Logic STEM Activity Book for Kids
books.author: Bob Mather
books.rationale: This book includes hours of education and outrageous fun through puzzles, engaging characters, and beginner coding activities.

books.title: Periodic Table of Elements 1000 Piece Puzzle
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: The Virtual Instructor (Art)
books.rationale: This is a wonderful art teacher resource for students.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Hoffman Academy
books.author: Joseph Hoffman
books.noteText: Free piano lessons are available online; premium membership is available ($20.00/mo.).

books.title: United States 1000 Piece Map Puzzle
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Wood Shop: Handy Skills and Creative Building Projects for Kids
books.author: Margaret Larson
books.rationale: With step-by-step photographs and clear instructions, aspiring woodworkers learn essential skills, such as how to drive a nail; use a power drill; “measure twice, cut once;” and saw correctly.

books.title: Will Kemp Art School
books.author: Will Kemp
books.rationale: This is a wonderful acrylic and oil art teacher resource for students.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Forest Craft: A Child's Guide to Whittling in the Woodland
books.author: Richard Irvine
books.rationale: This is a book that can be turned to time and time again, through the different seasons and over many years. The skills learned can be treasured and passed down through generations in time-honored traditions.
